jobs.title: Executive IT Support Engineer Intern (Summer 2026)
jobs.content_plain: Get to know Okta Okta is The World’s Identity Company. We free everyone to safely use any technology, anywhere, on any device or app. Our flexible and neutral products, Okta Platform and Auth0 Platform, provide secure access, authentication, and automation, placing identity at the core of business security and growth. At Okta, we celebrate a variety of perspectives and experiences. We are not looking for someone who checks every single box - we’re looking for lifelong learners and people who can make us better with their unique experiences.  Join our team! We’re building a world where Identity belongs to you. Location We’re seeking a dynamic, motivated contributor to join our team at one of our key office locations: San Francisco.  About the Internship The Premier Support Team at Okta is seeking an Executive IT Support Engineer Intern to assist with providing world-class technical support to our executives and VIP users. This role offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in IT operations, executive support, and cloud infrastructure while working closely with experienced IT professionals in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. The ideal candidate will be eager to learn, capable of troubleshooting complex issues, and passionate about working on high-profile projects. If you are passionate about IT, cloud technologies, and customer service, this internship offers hands-on experience with enterprise IT systems, cloud infrastructure, and the opportunity to drive impactful projects. You’ll gain valuable exposure to executive-level IT operations, mentorship from industry experts, and networking with key professionals.  What You’ll Get To Do Executive IT & VIP Support: Support Okta executives, Board members, and their Executive Assistants by troubleshooting hardware, software, identity and access management, and connectivity issues. Assist with device setup and lifecycle management (MacBooks, iPads, peripherals), and ensure executive readiness for meetings, travel, and high-visibility events. Help prepare and validate AV, conferencing, and workspace environments for critical meetings and leadership sessions. Leverage emerging AI-powered support tools to accelerate troubleshooting, surface knowledge, and improve resolution quality. Premier Support Operations, Escalations & Intelligent Triage: Participate in the Premier Support operating model by assisting with intake, prioritization, and resolution of executive IT requests. Learn how escalations are handled, how ownership is maintained across issues, and how high-impact problems are resolved quickly with minimal handoffs. Support the use of AI-assisted workflows for request triage, issue classification, and routing to improve response time and consistency. AI, Automation & Tooling Initiatives: Collaborate on strategic initiatives focused on AI-enabled support, automation, and next-generation service delivery. Assist with testing and improving AI-driven tools such as virtual support agents, automated workflows, and intelligent dashboards. Identify opportunities for AI to reduce manual work, enhance the executive experience, and improve operational efficiency through scripting, automation, or the integration of new technologies. Testing, Documentation & Knowledge Systems: Create and execute test cases for Premier Support workflows, automation, and AI-enabled tools to ensure reliability, security, and usability. Develop documentation, including user guides, internal runbooks, FAQs, and knowledge base articles. Contribute to building scalable, AI-supported knowledge systems that reduce repeat issues, improve first-contact resolution, and enable consistent white-glove service. Inventory, Logistics & Operational Support: Track IT assets and accessories, assist with device provisioning and redeployment, and support logistics for shipping, returns, and executive travel readiness. Work within platforms such as Jira and ServiceNow to manage tickets, tasks, and project work while learning how AI and automation can be applied to asset tracking, reporting, and operational workflows. Internship Capstone Project (AI or Automation-Focused): Lead a self-directed capstone project centered on improving an executive IT workflow, automating a recurring process, enhancing a support tool, or designing an AI-enabled solution. This may include building a prototype, improving an existing AI-driven process, or creating dashboards or documentation that demonstrate how AI can elevate executive IT support. Present your outcomes to the Premier Support leadership team and key stakeholders. Who We Are Looking For Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field and graduating December 2026 or Spring 2027. Knowledge of computer systems, hardware, and software troubleshooting. Basic understanding of networking concepts and security fundamentals. Familiarity with cloud computing concepts and tools like AWS, Docker, and Terraform is a plus. Strong communication and customer service skills, with a professional demeanor. Problem-solving skills with the ability to work autonomously and as part of a team.  Strong work ethic, attention to detail, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment. Experience with ITSM platforms such as Jira, ServiceNow, or Zendesk (preferred). Knowledge of scripting languages (e.g., Python, Bash) and automation tools (preferred). Curiosity about AI-powered tools, automation, and how emerging technologies can improve IT operations, support workflows, and the end-user experience. Comfort experimenting with new tools, learning quickly, and thinking about how processes can be made more efficient, scalable, and intelligent. Interest in staying up-to-date with evolving IT, cloud, and AI-enabled support trends (preferred). Okta’s Intern Program As an intern, you’ll do real work that matters. While you’re on board, you’ll work on meaningful projects and have an opportunity to see what working at Okta is all about. You’ll also have the assistance of your mentor and manager to help you develop new skills. Our interns have the opportunity to build a strong community - with their fellow interns, within their teams, and with the broader company. We want you to grow professionally, and you’ll do that through participating in events like our Executive Speaker Series and Brown Bags. And of course, we want you to have fun too.  We offer 12-week summer internships in the U.S. with two available start dates. You may choose from the following options: May 26 – August 14 or June 15 – September 4.   P24324_3339754 #LI-onsite Below is the annual base salary range for candidates located in San Francisco Bay Area. Your actual base salary will depend on factors such as your skills, qualifications, experience, and work location. In addition, Okta offers equity (where applicable), bonus, and benefits, including health, dental and vision insurance, 401(k), flexible spending account, and paid leave (including PTO and parental leave) in accordance with our applicable plans and policies. To learn more about our Total Rewards program please visit: https://rewards.okta.com/us .    The annual base salary range for this position for candidates located in the San Francisco Bay area is between: $26 — $26 USD What you can look forward to as a Full-Time Okta employee! Amazing Benefits Making Social Impact Developing Talent and Fostering Connection + Community at Okta Some roles may require travel to one of our office locations for in-person onboarding. Okta is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, ancestry, marital status, age, physical or mental disability, or status as a protected veteran. We also consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and convictions records, consistent with applicable laws. If reasonable accommodation is needed to complete any part of the job application, interview process, or onboarding please use this Form to request an accommodation. Notice for New York City Applicants & Employees: Okta may use Automated Employment Decision Tools (AEDT), as defined by New York City Local Law 144, that use artificial intelligence, machine learning, or other automated processes to assist in our recruitment and hiring process. In accordance with NYC Local Law 144, if you are an applicant or employee residing in New York City, please click here to view our full NYC AEDT Notice. Okta is committed to complying with applicable data privacy and security laws and regulations. For more information, please see our Personnel and Job Candidate Privacy Notice at  https://www.okta.com/legal/personnel-policy/.

jobs.title: Safety Research Internship (Spring/Summer 2026)
jobs.content_text:
Who are we?

Our mission is to scale intelligence to serve humanity. We’re training and deploying frontier models for developers and enterprises who are building AI systems to power magical experiences like content generation, semantic search, RAG, and agents. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. We like to work hard and move fast to do what’s best for our customers.

Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are passionate about their craft. Each person is one of the best in the world at what they do. We believe that a diverse range of perspectives is a requirement for building great products.

Join us on our mission and shape the future!

Why this role?

To have the opportunity to collaborate with Cohere’s Modelling Safety team on implementing novel research ideas that are grounded in real usage and real production models. We are broadly interested in the areas of:

  • Fairness

  • Safety: overall, but especially for multiple languages, dialects, and cultural contexts

  • Robustness

  • Generalisation

  • Interpretability

  • Safety for agents with complex read/write actions

  • Safety for codegen

If there is any area you would like to work on not covered by this, please feel free to propose it, that can succeed! To increase chances of success, we would recommend you familiarise yourself with the publications of the team to get signal on whether your topic is something we might be interested in.

The goal of an internship will be to publish a paper in a top venue and make a contribution to open science. Many internship projects do also make it into our production systems and influence our model development, though this is not a requirement, just a bonus. Your project details and topic will be collaboratively designed between you and the team.

Please Note: To be eligible for a Research Internship, you must be currently pursuing a PhD in Machine Learning, NLP, or a related discipline. You need to be available for a full-time internship that lasts for 4-6 months.

This role can be remote. We do allow and encourage working out of one of Cohere’s office locations, but do not provide relocation or housing if you are not already nearby.

As a Cohere Research Intern, you will:

  • Conduct cutting-edge machine learning research, training and evaluating production large language models.

  • Focus on research projects aimed at making models better understood, safer, more reliable, more inclusive, and more beneficial for the world

  • Disseminate your research results through the production of publications, datasets, and code.

  • Contribute to research initiatives that have practical applications in Cohere’s product development.

You may be a good fit if you:

  • Are currently pursuing, or in the process of obtaining, a PhD in Machine Learning, NLP, Artificial Intelligence, or a related discipline. We will also consider exceptional non-PhD candidates.

  • Are eligible for work authorization in the country of employment at the time of hire and maintain ongoing work authorization throughout the internship period.

  • Have experience using large-scale distributed training strategies, data annotation and evaluation pipelines, or implementing state of the art ML models.

  • Are familiar with autoregressive sequence models, such as Transformers.

  • Have strong communication and problem-solving skills with the ability to convey complex research findings clearly and succinctly.

  • Have knowledge, or are knowledgeable, of programming languages such as Python, C, C++, Lua, or related languages.

  • Have knowledge of related ML frameworks such as JAX, Pytorch and Tensorflow.

  • Have previous experience in building systems based on machine learning and deep learning techniques.

  • Demonstrate passion for applied NLP models and products.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Demonstrated expertise through publications in top tier venues in fields such as machine learning, NLP, artificial intelligence, computer vision, optimization, computer science, statistics, applied mathematics, or data science.

  • Proven ability to tackle analytical problems using quantitative methodologies.

  • Proficiency in handling and analysing complex, high-dimensional data from various sources.

  • Experience in applying theoretical and empirical research to real-world problem-solving.

If some of the above doesn’t line up perfectly with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Full-Time Employees at Cohere enjoy these Perks:

🤝 An open and inclusive culture and work environment 

🧑‍💻 Work closely with a team on the cutting edge of AI research 

🍽 Weekly lunch stipend, in-office lunches & snacks

🦷 Full health and dental benefits, including a separate budget to take care of your mental health 

🐣 100% Parental Leave top-up for up to 6 months

🎨 Personal enrichment benefits towards arts and culture, fitness and well-being, quality time, and workspace improvement

🏙 Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend

✈️ 6 weeks of vacation (30 working days!)
